reasoning: The prompt states the market price on November 27 was 0.09. This market likely asks whether Israel will strike Iran *again* before 2026 (since the major October 25, 2024 strikes already occurred). The 0.09 baseline is a strong anchor for the remaining time in 2025 (or late 2024). Over the past 10 days since Nov 27, there have been no major escalations with Iran (Lebanon ceasefire is holding, Syrian rebels are advancing but no direct Israeli strikes on Iran). Therefore, the probability should remain close to the recent market estimate of 9-10%. I estimate it at 10% because tail risks remain but the immediate trend is de-escalation compared to October.
